intro: Auricula minima, also known as Mouse Ear, is a small, delicate herb belonging to the Primulaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Auricula minima contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Auricula minima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions.
